title: Unit B-1 Self-Check (3 questions, for confirming understanding -- no pass/fail record is saved)

questions.q: What is the basic mindset for approaching generative AI when the first output doesn't match what you expected?
choices: A prompt must be written perfectly the first time -- if it fails, there's no way to try again
The basic approach is interactive: keep giving additional instructions on the output to improve it
An unexpected output means something is broken, so you should stop and contact support instead of trying anything further
Resending the same question in a more forceful tone improves the output
questions.explanation: Generative AI is not "a machine that gives the right answer in one shot"; it's a tool whose output you grow through dialogue. The basic approach is to treat the first output as a rough draft, tell the AI what's missing, and repeat the process of improvement.

questions.q: Which of the following is the most appropriate example of providing context (background information)?
choices: Saying, "The audience is 10 first-year new hires, the time limit is 30 minutes, and the goal is learning the procedure"
Repeating polite greetings such as "thank you very much in advance, I truly appreciate your help" without ever stating what you actually need
Urging it along with "please hurry and finish this as fast as possible", mentioning nothing but the time pressure
Describing, in detail, a personal hobby or how you spend your days off, unrelated to the audience or purpose
questions.explanation: Context refers to the background information and constraints -- such as the audience, purpose, time, and length -- that determine the direction of the output. Greetings or requests for speed don't help tailor the content of the output to your workplace.

questions.q: Which of the following follow-up instructions is most likely to lead to a real improvement in the output?
choices: Saying, "please make it better", without stating what specifically should change
Giving a vague reaction, "something's off", without ever explaining why
Saying, "the second item is too hard for the audience -- please use plain language instead of technical terms"
Saying, "please redo the whole thing", without specifying anything that should change
questions.explanation: The more specifically you state "what" is wrong and "how" you'd like it fixed, the more targeted the improvement will be. With "make it better," "something's off," or "redo the whole thing," the AI can only guess at what to change.
